_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to look at health messages in our community and think about what they are really trying to tell us.

_source.w: We are learning to look at health messages in our community and think about what they are really trying to tell us.
y: Health messages are everywhere — learning to interpret them helps us make informed choices.
Not all health messages are equally reliable — thinking critically protects us.
Understanding the purpose behind messages helps us decide which ones to follow.
eg: Analysing a food advertisement and identifying what it is really trying to get us to do.
Comparing health information from different sources — school, internet, TV.
Discussing the difference between health facts and marketing claims.
Investigating who creates the health messages we see and why.
Looking at health messages aimed at different age groups and discussing the differences.
Evaluating a community health campaign and discussing whether its message is trustworthy.
